summary: LiteLLM PyPI supply chain compromise deploys multi-stage credential stealer
details: TeamPCP threat group compromised the LiteLLM package on PyPI (March 2026) through a cascading supply chain attack via Aqua Security's Trivy and Checkmarx KICS. Malicious LiteLLM versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 contained a multi-stage credential stealer targeting SSH keys, cloud tokens, Kubernetes secrets, crypto wallets, and .env files, plus a persistent systemd backdoor. The package has 3.4 million downloads per day and is used by Stripe, Netflix, Google, CrewAI, DSPy, and MLflow.

agent_threat.attack_vector: Cascading CI/CD compromise through trusted security tools, deploying credential stealer via popular AI proxy library
mitigations: Pin package versions and verify hashes before install
Use mcp-secure signModel() to verify package provenance
Monitor for unexpected systemd services after package updates
